questions.question: What is a queue data structure?
options: A) A data structure that follows the First-In-First-Out (FIFO) principle
B) A data structure that follows the Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) principle
C) A data structure that allows elements to be added or removed at any position
D) A data structure that allows elements to be added or removed from both ends
questions.explanation: A queue follows the First-In-First-Out (FIFO) principle, meaning the first element added to the queue will be the first one to be removed.

questions.question: What is the main difference between a queue and a stack?
options: A) A queue can only be used when the order of elements matters
B) A stack follows the First-In-First-Out (FIFO) principle, while a queue follows the Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) principle
C) A queue can only be used with dynamic arrays
D) A stack can only be used for removing elements from both ends
questions.explanation: A stack follows the Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) principle, meaning the last element added to the stack will be the first one to be removed. A queue, on the other hand, follows the First-In-First-Out (FIFO) principle.

questions.question: What is a circular queue?
options: A) A queue that uses a dynamic array to store elements
B) A queue that allows elements to be added or removed from both ends
C) A queue that wraps around to the beginning when the last element is reached
D) A queue that allows elements to be removed from the middle
questions.explanation: A circular queue is a type of queue that wraps around to the beginning when the last element is reached, allowing it to use a dynamic array efficiently.

questions.question: What is the advantage of using a dynamic array to implement a queue?
options: A) It allows for more efficient removal of elements from the middle
B) It allows for more efficient addition of elements to the end
C) It allows for more efficient usage of memory
D) It allows for more efficient implementation of the queue data structure
questions.explanation: Using a dynamic array to implement a queue allows for more efficient usage of memory, as the array can be resized dynamically to accommodate changing element counts.

questions.question: What is the term for a data structure that allows multiple stacks to be used concurrently?
options: A) Multiple queues
B) Multiple stacks
C) Multiple arrays
D) Multiple sets
questions.explanation: A multiple stack is a data structure that allows multiple stacks to be used concurrently, allowing for efficient and flexible implementation of stack operations.
